message.text: User-supplied 'next' query parameter is passed directly to Flask's redirect() without any URL validation or domain allowlisting. An attacker can craft a link such as /api/redirect?next=https://evil.com to redirect users to a malicious site via a trusted domain, enabling credential phishing and OAuth token theft.

No validation is performed to ensure the redirect target is a relative path or belongs to a trusted domain allowlist. The endpoint accepts arbitrary schemes (javascript:, data:, https://) enabling both phishing redirects and potential XSS via javascript: URIs in older browsers.
